details: Package ships only README.md and a minimal package.json with no main entry, no bin, no lifecycle scripts, and no executable code. The README self-identifies as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept ("Takeover By L0bo") and the package.json description reads "POC ONLY FOR TIKTOK". No code runs at install or import time, so there is no direct exfiltration, RCE, or credential-theft mechanism. The risk is purely namespace-level: an organization that has an internal package by this name could resolve to this public placeholder instead of the intended internal artifact, allowing the squatter to later replace the contents with malicious code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
